source.title: ZTO Express Q4 2025 & FY2025 Earnings Results

extractions.claim: ZTO Express FY2025 parcel volume was 38.52 billion, up 13.3% YoY, missing its own 20-24% growth guidance of 40.8-42.2 billion parcels.
extractions.evidence: ZTO reported a full-year parcel volume of 38.52 billion parcels in 2025, a 13.3% year-over-year increase. The company had targeted a volume of between 40.8 billion and 42.2 billion parcel deliveries for 2025, representing 20% to 24% growth.

verification.verifier_notes: FY2025 actual vs guidance from ZTO earnings release and Morningstar/Nasdaq reporting

extractions.claim: ZTO Q4 2025 parcel volume was 10.56 billion, up 9.2% YoY, with revenue up 12.3% to CNY 14.5 billion.
extractions.evidence: In Q4 2025, ZTO reported parcel volume of 10.56 billion, up 9.2% year-over-year. Total revenue rose 12.3% to CNY 14.5 billion in the fourth quarter.

verification.verifier_notes: Q4 results from ZTO earnings release

extractions.claim: ZTO FY2025 gross margin declined 6.0 percentage points to 25.0%, with full-year adjusted net income of CNY 9.5 billion.
extractions.evidence: Gross margin down 6.0 points to 25.0% for 2025. Adjusted net income was CNY 9.5 billion for the full year.

verification.verifier_notes: From ZTO earnings release and multiple financial reporting sources

extractions.claim: ZTO's 2026 parcel volume guidance is 10-13% growth, a significant step-down from 2025's 20%+ target, signaling a pivot from volume to quality and shareholder returns.
extractions.evidence: ZTO has given a guidance of growth between 10%-13% for 2026. Despite core adjusted EBITDA margins declining 650 basis points year-over-year, the company emphasized higher shareholder returns with a new $1.5 billion buyback program and targeting aggregate annual returns of no less than 50% of prior year adjusted net income.

verification.verifier_notes: 2026 guidance from ZTO Q4 earnings call, confirmed by Morningstar and StockTitan

extractions.claim: ZTO's market share dropped from 22.9% in 2023 to 19.4% in 2024, falling below 20% for the first time since 2020.
extractions.evidence: ZTO's market share dropped from 22.9% in 2023 to 19.4% in 2024, falling below the 20% mark for the first time since 2020. ZTO sacrificed some market share in exchange for better profits.

verification.verifier_notes: Market share data from BambooWorks analysis based on industry volume and ZTO disclosures
